items.headline: OSE Immunotherapeutics turns to IRIS for €19.3m bridge financing
items.preview: OSE Immunotherapeutics has secured a €19.3 million financing deal with IRIS Capital.
items.article:
French immunotherapy developer OSE Immunotherapeutics has signed a term sheet for a flexible bridge equity financing with IRIS Capital Investment, giving the Nantes- and Paris-based biotech access to up to €19.3 million in gross proceeds over 24 months.
The planned financing, structured through IRIS’s SmartATM mechanism, would allow IRIS to subscribe for up to 4 million new OSE shares, corresponding to around 17% of the company’s current share capital. Why this matters: This financing is crucial for OSE as it seeks to extend its runway while exploring other funding options and strategic partnerships.
The immediate financial context is tight. OSE reported €17.0 million in cash and cash equivalents at the end of March 2026 and stated that it does not currently have sufficient net working capital to meet its obligations over the next 12 months. If fully used, the IRIS facility would extend its cash runway only to the end of December 2026, based on current plans and excluding any potential future milestone payments.

items.headline: GSK reports 19% functional cure rate for near-approval hepatitis B drug
items.preview: GSK's hepatitis B drug candidate shows a 19% functional cure rate in Phase III trials.
items.article:
GSK has linked its chronic hepatitis B drug candidate to a 19% functional cure rate across a pair of Phase III trials, clearing the bar it set for clinical relevance.
The placebo-controlled trials assessed the ability of bepirovirsen, an antisense oligonucleotide targeting hepatitis B transcripts, to cause functional cures in adults with noncirrhotic chronic infections. Why this matters: The results indicate a promising new treatment option for chronic hepatitis B, potentially impacting a significant patient population.
The FDA has accepted a filing for approval of bepirovirsen for review, positioning GSK to secure clearance to launch the drug in the US in late October. GSK has forecast non-risk adjusted peak year sales of more than £2 billion.

items.headline: Unraveling Spontaneous Hemorrhage: Breakthroughs Transform Treatment
items.preview: Innovative strategies are reshaping the treatment landscape for spontaneous intracerebral hemorrhage.
items.article:
The landscape of spontaneous intracerebral hemorrhage (ICH) treatment is undergoing a seismic transformation driven by rapid technological advances, innovative biological strategies, and the integration of precision medicine principles.
Minimally invasive surgery has emerged as a linchpin in this evolving paradigm, focusing on reducing surgical trauma while accelerating hematoma clearance and preserving vital brain tissue integrity. Why this matters: These advancements promise to significantly improve patient outcomes and reduce morbidity associated with ICH.
The field of mitochondrial biology has unveiled exciting prospects through techniques designed to repair damaged mitochondria or facilitate their intercellular transfer. However, these strategies face substantial ethical and technical hurdles that must be carefully navigated to ensure safe translational applications.

items.headline: Playing defense is a losing strategy: The U.S. needs an offensive biopharma plan
items.preview: A new commentary argues for a proactive U.S. biopharma strategy to maintain leadership.
items.article:
The U.S. still leads in biopharma innovation, but defensive policy alone will lose it. The greatest threat may not be Chinese competition itself, but the defensive crouch Washington has adopted in response.
By enacting the highly restrictive Biosecure Act, Washington has moved decisively toward a defense-first biopharma strategy. Why this matters: A shift to an offensive strategy is essential to maintain U.S. leadership in biopharmaceutical innovation and attract global talent.
An offensive strategy must replace the current piecemeal approach, focusing on scaling domestic capacity, capturing global talent, and calibrated oversight to ensure the U.S. remains a leader in drug development.

items.headline: Beyond reproduction: The ovary as a systemic regulator of female health and aging
items.preview: New research highlights the ovary's role beyond reproduction in female health and aging.
items.article:
Ovarian function directly impacts female health from childhood to old age, with systemic consequences that propagate across various body systems. The dominant clinical paradigm has inadequately addressed the implications of ovarian aging on overall health.
This reframing matters for how we interpret the data on aging we currently have. Why this matters: Understanding the systemic role of the ovary could lead to novel therapeutic approaches for age-related health issues in women.
The field has also been looking at the wrong point in time—ovarian aging is not a perimenopausal phenomenon but begins much earlier, affecting health trajectories throughout a woman's life.
